title: TOCTOU in SSRF guard: DNS checked then re-resolved
description: actor_cache.rs:48 calls is_private_host_resolved(host) which resolves DNS and checks for private IPs. Then at line 57, self.client.get(actor_uri) resolves DNS again for the actual HTTP request. Between these two DNS resolutions, an attacker controlling their DNS server can return a public IP for the check and a private IP (e.g., 169.254.169.254 for cloud metadata) for the actual fetch. This is a classic DNS rebinding / TOCTOU attack. The same pattern exists in server.rs:718-732 (Follow handler fetches follower actor), delivery.rs:150-153 (delivery worker re-validates then sends), and media.rs:54-66 (media fetch). The fix is to resolve DNS once and pin the resolved IP for the actual request, or use a custom DNS resolver that filters private IPs. This is a confidentiality/integrity issue: SSRF to internal services. Locations: actor_cache.rs:42-61, server.rs:718-753, delivery.rs:143-154, media.rs:53-66.
close_reason: Duplicate of broadside-goj2.19 which was filed by a prior review pass
